hero.title: Designing the future
hero.description: Hi, I'm {name}. A UX Strategist and Frontend Developer creating modular, user-centered digital ecosystems.

work.title: Selected Projects.
work.subtitle: A showcase of strategy, design, and implementation.

paquita-pilates.title: Paquita Pilates — Website & Booking Platform
paquita-pilates.description: An instructor with deep expertise and a down-to-earth teaching style — but a digital presence falling behind. For paquitapilatesreformer.com, I developed brand identity, UX architecture, and a custom booking system that feels as personal as walking through her studio door.
paquita-pilates.longDescription: Paquita brings years of training, her own methodology, and genuine character to every class. What was missing was a digital experience matching that depth. I led the complete relaunch: brand discovery, UX architecture, UI design, and full-stack implementation. The result: paquitapilatesreformer.com — a booking platform that doesn't feel like off-the-shelf software. Real-time class scheduling, credit wallet system, role-based dashboards for clients and instructors. Every screen designed to feel warm and unhurried — nothing like SaaS, everything like the studio itself. Built with Vue.js, Ionic, and a custom Node/MongoDB API.

gallery.1: Home screen — class schedule, credit balance, and instant booking in one view
gallery.2: Studio dashboard — desktop view for managing classes, clients, and schedules

1.title: Smart Scheduling
1.desc: Real-time availability with instant confirmation — no back-and-forth, just flow.

2.title: Seamless Anywhere
2.desc: From desktop to smartphone, the experience stays elegant and intuitive.

3.title: Studio Identity
3.desc: Visual language reflecting the authentic warmth and earthy tones of the physical space.

4.title: Dual Perspectives
4.desc: Separate interfaces for clients and instructors, each optimized for their needs.

hiqer.title: HIQER — Persuasion for the Outdoors
hiqer.description: What if your phone made you put your phone away? HIQER turns outdoor exploration into a competitive quest — GPS triggers, XP, leaderboards, and a companion Watch app designed so the screen stays in your pocket.
hiqer.longDescription:
**The Challenge**

How can a device be used to connect physical nature exploration with digital added value in a way that creates long-term user engagement? The goal: increasing intrinsic motivation of hikers through digital design.

**1. Concept & Device Strategy**

The deliberate choice was the smartphone as the mobile link between user and environment. Through IoT approaches and location-based services, the hiking trail becomes interactive:

- **Object Networking:** QR codes at physical markers or GPS recognition connect the smartphone with each 'Spot'
- **Digital Layer in Nature:** The phone as a window to history — information about cultural sites, trail facts, or 'Then-vs-Now' comparisons available on-site

**2. Psychological Strategy (Persuasive Design)**

To maintain motivation continuously, HIQER uses targeted design mechanisms:

- **Cognitive Curiosity:** The 'thirst for knowledge' is satisfied through exclusive, location-bound content at destination points
- **Gamification & Rewards:** Experience points for reached spots, a level system enables Social Comparison — users compare progress and motivate each other
- **Ambition & Fitness:** Real-time fitness data (e.g. step counter) coupled with quests and social comparisons awaken personal ambition

**3. Synergy of Nature and Technology**

The concept goes beyond the purely digital layer and addresses physiological needs. Networking with local infrastructure offers discount codes for nearby restaurants — a targeted connection of extrinsic reward and real benefit after hiking.

**4. The Result**

HIQER transforms classic hiking into a connected experience. The smartphone shifts from distraction factor to essential tool for discovery, fitness, and social interaction in nature.

**My Methodology:** Conducted guerrilla field tests, mapped three distinct personas, iterative prototype development until friction disappeared. The Apple Watch companion was the key insight — glanceable cues and haptic feedback meant the phone never needed to leave the pocket. Highest marks. Presented at the HdM Digital Innovation Forum.

gallery.1: Quest map — GPS-triggered outdoor challenges scattered across the city
gallery.2: Active quests — XP tracking, progress bars, and upcoming rewards
gallery.3: Leaderboard — social ranking to spark friendly competition between explorers
gallery.4: Apple Watch companion — glanceable navigation and haptic cues, phone stays in your pocket

1.title: Location Triggers
1.desc: QR spots and GPS unlock contextual content exactly where curiosity strikes.

2.title: Playful Progress
2.desc: XP, levels, and leaderboards transform every walk into an achievement.

3.title: Cross-Device Flow
3.desc: Smartwatch navigation means your phone stays in your pocket while exploring.

4.title: Curiosity Engine
4.desc: Historical storytelling and local secrets revealed at exact coordinates.

kischde.title: Kischde — Trade Within Trust
kischde.description: No marketplace noise. No strangers. Just a circle of people you trust — and the things you no longer need. Kischde is a swipe-first trading app built on the belief that giving should feel better than selling.
kischde.longDescription: Five people. One tight deadline. Real deployment ambitions. Kischde was everything a real product launch feels like — pressure, tradeoffs, and the constant pull between what we wanted and what we had time to build. I led UX system design and owned a large portion of the Vue.js/Ionic frontend. The core challenge: make trading feel nothing like a marketplace. The answer was circles — you only see items from people you've invited, stripping away the anxiety of public listings. Swipe-first browsing kept cognitive load near zero. The backend was built to scale without over-engineering the MVP.

gallery.1: Home feed — items shared within your trusted circle, no marketplace noise
gallery.2: Profile view — your listings, exchange history, and trust connections

1.title: Circle-Based
1.desc: Exchange only within trusted friend groups — familiar faces, zero friction.

2.title: Circular by Design
2.desc: Every trade extends product lifecycles and reduces waste naturally.

3.title: Mobile-First
3.desc: Platform-agnostic architecture. Your items, always within reach.

4.title: Effortless Flow
4.desc: Intuitive gestures for giving and requesting — no forms, no friction.

steal-back.title: Steal Back — Play as Protest
steal-back.description: A 2D pixel-art puzzle platformer where activism meets gameplay. Uncover colonial art theft by day, reclaim history by night. Based on true events that demand to be remembered.
steal-back.longDescription: Steal Back is a game concept that emerged from a research project on colonial art restitution. Rather than write a paper, I designed a playable argument. The day/night cycle is the mechanic: during the day, the player investigates — reading archives, speaking to NPCs, building a case. At night, they act — using stealth and puzzle-solving to recover the stolen objects and return them. Each piece you reclaim triggers a factual summary of the real theft it represents. The pixel art style was chosen deliberately — it codes as 'safe and playful' while carrying genuinely heavy subject matter.

1.title: Day & Night
1.desc: Information gathering transforms into stealth action as darkness falls.

2.title: Puzzle Mastery
2.desc: Environmental challenges demanding strategy, timing, and creativity.

3.title: Historical Grounding
3.desc: Every artwork tells a true colonial story — play to remember.

4.title: Pixel Artistry
4.desc: Hand-crafted retro aesthetics with modern depth and atmosphere.

beast.title: B.E.A.S.T. — Charging with Character for Mercedes-AMG
beast.description: How do you bring heartbeat back to electric vehicles? In a Winter 2022/23 interdisciplinary project, our HdM Stuttgart team collaborated with Mercedes-AMG to prototype multimodal charging experiences — light, haptics, and sound — validated through real user testing in the Mobile Lab.
beast.longDescription:
**The Problem: The 'Ping' Paradox.**

EV charging is silent. Plug in, beep, wait. The emotional heartbeat that combustion engines provided — the roar, the vibration, the feeling of power — is completely absent. For AMG customers, this creates a gap in brand perception.

**1. Analysis & Briefing**

Together with Mercedes-AMG and Mercedes-Benz, we defined a clear mission for the Winter 2022/23 semester: make the car feel 'alive' during charging. Our target group analysis identified two archetypes: the 'Performer' (status-oriented, tech-savvy, efficiency-focused) and the 'Hedonist' (experience-oriented, comfort-seeking, trend-conscious).

**2. Ideation & Prototyping**

Our interdisciplinary HdM team developed three sound categories: 'BEAST' (organic, animalistic, heartbeat sounds), 'PING' (comfortable, familiar, chimes), and 'Futuristic' (sci-fi, synthetic, energetic). In parallel, we designed lighting concepts — from fiery red (BEAST) to blue energy flow (Futuristic).

The technical implementation was ambitious: LED animations via ESP32 controllers for grille and side lights, haptic feedback through magnets at the charging connector (the 'suction effect'), plus proximity detection via scanners and tags.

**3. Testing Methodology**

We conducted two separate testing phases: Card-sorting with 19 participants to categorize sounds (sorting 20 sounds into charging phases) and UEQ-S lab tests with 15 users in the Mobile Lab. We evaluated attribute pairs like obstructive vs. supportive, complicated vs. simple, boring vs. exciting.

**4. Results & Insights**

The results surprised us: The futuristic lighting concept (blue energy flow from charging port into car) achieved the highest scores for pragmatism (M=1.767) and hedonics (M=1.926). BEAST sounds were often perceived as 'creepy' or outside the comfort zone — only 55% could categorize them correctly.

Color psychology played a decisive role: users intuitively associate blue with electricity and energy flow, while red signals danger or 'stop'. The Performer persona recognized futuristic sounds 100% of the time, while Hedonists were more open to familiar 'ping' sounds.

**5. Reflection & Recommendations**

The 'Perfect Experience' according to our findings: blue light animation (Futuristic) combined with subtle BEAST plug-in sound. Haptic feedback was rated neutral to positive but needs refinement.

Users want personalization — volume control, off-switch, individual charging status displays. Methodologically, one limitation remains: our test group (predominantly 18-25 year old students) doesn't exactly represent the Mercedes-AMG target demographic.

**My Contribution:** UX research and conceptual design. Together with teammates from sound design and technical engineering. 60-page research documentation. Presented to AMG leadership.

1.title: Light Choreography
1.desc: Visual charging progress synced with audio — an ambient light show.

2.title: Haptic Signature
2.desc: Vibration patterns matching AMG's pulse, felt through the cable.

3.title: Signature Sound
3.desc: Emotive audio landscapes unique to AMG — the heartbeat reborn.

4.title: Sensed Progress
4.desc: Multisensory feedback transforms waiting into an immersive experience.

emobility.title: Connected E-Mobility — One Ecosystem, One Interface
emobility.description: What if your car, wallbox, solar panels, and home grid finally spoke the same language? My bachelor thesis (graded 1.0) developed a connected e-mobility interface giving Mercedes customers the unified control their ecosystem deserves.
emobility.longDescription: Mercedes customers with a complete EQ ecosystem — vehicle, wallbox, solar, home energy — were juggling disconnected apps. My thesis asked: what if one intelligent interface connected everything? Developed in cooperation with Mercedes-Benz Customer Solutions, I designed the answer — from information architecture to high-fidelity mockups: Dynamic Island integration for live charging status, an energy dashboard for complete overview, intelligent departure planning, and a V2L control center. Twelve competitors analyzed, expert interviews conducted, every design decision grounded in research. Graded 1.0.

gallery.1: Home screen — vehicle status, charge level, and energy overview at a glance
gallery.2: Dynamic Island integration — live charging state visible without opening the app
gallery.3: Energy dashboard — home, wallbox, and grid connected in one unified view
gallery.4: Smart charging — departure-time planning optimises cost and green energy usage
gallery.5: Modular menu — each function is its own widget, scaling from glance to full control

1.title: Unified Control
1.desc: One interface for all AMG EQ products — seamless switching, zero confusion.

2.title: Energy Intelligence
2.desc: Smart optimization across devices, from vehicle to wallbox to grid.

3.title: Modular Scales
3.desc: From compact widgets to full dashboards — information density adapts to context.

4.title: Future-Ready
4.desc: Foundation architecture for next-generation connected mobility experiences.

ux-design.title: UX Design & Research
ux-design.description: Research-backed concept generation. Transforming user needs and data into seamless, persuasive interfaces.

frontend.title: Frontend Development
frontend.description: Clean, modular technical implementation of design systems. Building fast, responsive, and maintainable applications.

backend.title: Backend & Systems
backend.description: Bridging the gap between front-end UI and server architecture to ensure end-to-end functionality and performance.

dhbw.description: Development of digitalization concepts for various departments of the Baden-Württemberg Cooperative State University. Project manager for the further development of the campus management system.

mercedes-student.description: Design of user interfaces (UI concepts) for automotive group product platforms in the field of digital innovations — direct influence on the bachelor thesis on e-mobility.

mercedes-intern.description: Design of UI/UX concepts for the infotainment system of the vehicles "Mercedes-Benz User Experience" (MBUX) in the entertainment sector — basis for the later B.E.A.S.T. project.

travel.description: Worked at Cosmo Elqui Hostel in La Serena, stayed with a Chilean host family, and traveled through Chile and southern Peru — formative experience for intercultural thinking and design empathy.

contact.title: Let's connect.
